title: Determining methylation status of methylguanine DNA methyl transferase (MGMT) from formalin-fixed, paraffin embedded tumor tissue
author: de Abreu, Gallagher, et al.

steps.title: Run method
steps.instruction: •lick on the “Run Method” icon on the left hand side of the window.•lick on “Open Run Method”.•Select “45 cycles” and click on “OK”.•Verify that the reaction volume is set to 20 mcl.•Verify the thermal profile, as shown in Table 5, and edit if necessary.•Click on the “Start Run” icon on the top right hand corner of the screen.•Be sure to save the run as a unique file name.

steps.title: Data analysis
steps.instruction: When the run has completed, the software should automatically open the “Analysis” window.•In the Analysis window with all samples selected, verify the efficiency of each standard curve for MGMT and ACTB. The value should be between 90 and 110%.•Verify the threshold already established by the software. Make sure that all samples and controls have the same threshold.

steps.title: Data analysis
steps.instruction: The threshold should be in the middle of the exponential curve.•Then click on the “Analysis” icon on the left hand side of the screen.•Export the results to an excel file.

steps.title: Data analysis
steps.instruction: Select “Export”, then “Browse” → “Desktop” → “MGMT” folder. After that, select “Start Export”. The excel file with the data will be exported to the Ion folder, and then you can close the window.•Save the excel file generated by the software. Copy the “t” and “Quantity mean” values of each sample from the “Analysis” file and paste them onto the “MGMT Worksheet”.•Automatically the PMR (Percentage of Methylated Reference) will be calculated.
